title: Second-derivative UV Spectroscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1lvt3","text":"Second-derivative spectroscopy has proven to be an effective analytical tool because of its ability to resolve overlapping bands in the normal spectrum.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eml9h","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ritu","text":"This protocol is used to determine the degree of tyrosine exposure in proteins using second-derivative UV spectroscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Determine protein concentrations either using absorption coefficients (Abs @ 280 nm) or using BCA, Bradford, etc. colorimetric assays. Proteins should be used at concentration where A 280 ~ 0.2

Copy UV scan data from instrument into a Prism file with columns for wavelength and absorbance values. Analyze>XY Analysis> Smooth, Differentiate, or Integrate Curve>Create Second Derivative of Curve>smooth or not? Prism Analyze >XY Analysis > Smooth, Differentiate, or Integrate Curve >Create Second Derivative of Curve >smooth or not? Prism
creator: Moriah Beck
